design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from 'Order food with AI' + use_case + audience + generated_at produced workflow-command-center architecture. AIDA Check: Hero (Attention) → proof + bento (Interest) → motion workflow + guide (Desire) → CTA band (Action). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk. Hero Math Verification: clamp headline width keeps 2–3 lines at common breakpoints. Bento Density Verification: grid-auto-flow:dense present. GSAP Motion Selection: pinned-chapter-scroll + hover-physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: all CTAs point to Super login URL, black/white contrast verified.
headline: Order food with AI — a personal agent that actually places the order

site_brief.audience: people who want an AI assistant to order meals, groceries, and delivery without babysitting apps
site_brief.goal: Create an indexable SEO use-case page in the /use-cases/ cluster. Target the practical query 'Order food with AI', show the exact job-to-be-done, and explain how Super can perform the workflow with real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache. Include sibling use-case links so the cluster feels intentional, crawlable, and useful. Add a long-form operator playbook with how-to steps, setup checklist, realistic limits, confirmation moments, and examples that a reader can act on immediately.
queries: computer use AI agent ordering food
site_brief.title: Order food with AI
site_brief.use_case_intent: show how Super can coordinate food ordering across delivery apps, preferences, addresses, payment confirmation, and repeat orders

summary: This use‑case guide explains how to order meals and groceries with an AI assistant that operates real apps, confirms details, and remembers repeat orders using a computer-use cache. It’s written for people who are tired of juggling delivery apps and want a durable, repeatable workflow instead of a one‑off chatbot demo.
